zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Smithe於1596年10月18日出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，她的父親Michael Thome Smithe31歲，母親Helen McKay30歲。她於1637年10月21日與John Cookney在英國英格蘭維爾爾梅爾可善姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1644年4月18日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Smithe于1596年10月18日出生在英国英格兰戴文时，她的父亲Michael Thome Smithe31岁，母亲Helen McKay30岁。她于1637年10月21日与John Cookney在英国英格兰維爾爾梅爾可善姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1644年4月18日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Smithe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Smithe was born on 18 October 1596, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, Michael Thome Smithe, was 31 and her mother, Helen McKay, was 30. She married John Cookney on 21 October 1637, in Melksham, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 18 April 1644, in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Smithe est née le 18 octobre 1596, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Michael Thome Smithe, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Helen McKay, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Cookney le 21 octobre 1637, à Melksham, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 18 avril 1644, à Henrico, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 47 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Smithe(이)가 1596년 10월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Michael Thome Smithe(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helen McKay(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1637년 10월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 멜크샤에서 John Cookney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1644년 4월 18일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 헨리코에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Smithe родилась 18 октября 1596 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Michael Thome Smithe было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Helen McKay, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cookney 21 октября 1637 г. в местности мелкшам, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 18 апреля 1644 г. в местности хенрику, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 47 лет.
